caveats: a: The BLS industry group pools tobacco with unrelated paper, petroleum, farm-supply, and other wholesalers.
a: O*NET task importance is not time, wage weight, current automation, or technical substitutability.
a: The frozen labor ratio mixes 2024 wages with 2022 receipts, and high pass-through product value makes revenue a particularly large denominator.
rho: No direct source measures five-year AI implementation in compliant 424940 firms.
rho: Regulatory work can be assisted but not safely eliminated without accountable review.
e: FDA authorization counts and enforcement activity do not reveal the share of 424940 firms that are materially compliant.
e: The 590-firm band estimate uses a generic distributor margin bridge that may be poorly matched to high-revenue, low-margin tobacco distribution.
s5: The observed transaction source covers all nondurable wholesalers and only deals reported to its marketplace.
s5: No exact denominator, owner-age distribution, or failed-deal dataset exists for the eligible lens.
q: FTC promotion data are manufacturer-side, dated, and specific to reporting e-cigarette companies rather than all 424940 contracts.
q: Capture can differ sharply among cigarettes, cigars, oral nicotine, authorized electronic cigarettes, and independent retail customers.
d5: Product-category dollar growth is not constant-price quantity and categories have very different units.
d5: Unauthorized electronic-cigarette sales make measured market totals incomplete and legally non-durable.
d5: New federal or state flavor, nicotine, tax, postal, or authorization rules could move the five-year basket substantially.
o: Regulation preserves accountable functions but does not guarantee that an independent LMM wholesaler performs them.
o: The operator-required share is likely lower for national retail chains and higher for fragmented convenience and specialty stores.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: High-frequency B2B product sales earning thin wholesale spreads and manufacturer promotional allowances, with value added through assortment, credit, tax and regulatory compliance, inventory availability, route delivery, retailer service, and transaction records.
lens.deviation_from_default: Narrowed to materially compliant distribution because firms dependent on unauthorized or illicit electronic-cigarette products have a fundamentally different enforcement, inventory-seizure, and transferability profile that would make one screen incoherent.
lens.excluded_activities: Manufacturer-owned captive distribution, pure retailing, pure manufacturing, non-inventory brokers, businesses materially dependent on unauthorized or illicit products, shells, and firms outside the approximately $1-10M normalized EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: Materially compliant independent merchant wholesalers repeatedly sourcing, holding, selling, delivering, and documenting authorized cigarettes, cigars, smokeless tobacco, nicotine pouches, electronic cigarettes, and related products for external convenience, tobacco-specialty, grocery, fuel, hospitality, and other licensed retail customers.

a.bridge: BLS reports occupations for the much broader 4241/4247/4249 nondurable-wholesaler group, and O*NET describes generic wholesale-sales tasks rather than wage-weighted time. I map order intake, availability and price responses, promotional reconciliation, reporting, sales support, credit, and compliance-document preparation to AI-assistable work, then discount for route delivery, warehouse movement, stocking, relationship selling, regulated approvals, and existing EDI automation. The proxy may overstate exposure if tobacco distribution is more route- and warehouse-heavy, or understate it if its unusually intensive reporting and promotion administration dominates office wages.
caveats: The BLS industry group pools tobacco with unrelated paper, petroleum, farm-supply, and other wholesalers.
O*NET task importance is not time, wage weight, current automation, or technical substitutability.
The frozen labor ratio mixes 2024 wages with 2022 receipts, and high pass-through product value makes revenue a particularly large denominator.
a.change_evidence: A 424940-specific SOC-by-loaded-wage payroll extract and task-time study across compliant distributors, including current EDI adoption, sales administration, excise-tax and PACT reporting, warehouse, route, merchandising, and customer-service work.
a.rationale: AI can assist retailer inquiries, order and invoice exceptions, promotion and rebate reconciliation, customer-specific assortment, route and replenishment planning, accounts receivable, product-authorization checks, and required reporting. But broad BLS data show sales, driving, material moving, and stocking as major jobs, while the extremely low frozen labor ratio indicates that even meaningful task exposure sits on a small labor base.

d5.bridge: FTC and CDC series measure manufacturer sales, retail dollar sales, and consumer prevalence for separate products, not constant-price wholesaler service quantity for the current 424940 basket. I blend continued cigarette and smokeless decline with growth and substitution into electronic cigarettes and nicotine pouches that remain inside the code. Dollar-series growth likely overstates quantity because of price and product-mix effects, while exclusion of fragmented or unauthorized electronic-cigarette sales may understate channel volume.
caveats: Product-category dollar growth is not constant-price quantity and categories have very different units.
Unauthorized electronic-cigarette sales make measured market totals incomplete and legally non-durable.
New federal or state flavor, nicotine, tax, postal, or authorization rules could move the five-year basket substantially.
d5.change_evidence: Five years of inflation-adjusted 424940 shipment units, active SKUs, and revenue split among cigarettes, cigars, traditional smokeless, nicotine pouches, authorized electronic cigarettes, and other products, reconciled to retailer scan data and regulatory removals.
d5.rationale: The combustible core is contracting: FTC reported cigarettes sold to wholesalers and retailers fell from 190.2 billion in 2021 to 173.5 billion in 2022, and CDC reports cigarette packs down 27% from 2015 to 2021. Offsetting categories grew rapidly: CDC reports monthly e-cigarette sales rising from $75 million in January 2017 to $469 million in March 2022 and nicotine lozenge, puck, and pouch sales more than doubling from $452.76 million in 2020 to $1.06 billion in 2022. The blended basket therefore likely contracts moderately, with wide regulatory and substitution uncertainty.

caveats: FDA authorization counts and enforcement activity do not reveal the share of 424940 firms that are materially compliant.
The 590-firm band estimate uses a generic distributor margin bridge that may be poorly matched to high-revenue, low-margin tobacco distribution.
e.change_evidence: A verified roster of the estimated band firms matched to ownership, state licenses, ATF registrations, FDA-authorized product mix, enforcement history, external-customer revenue, repeat-order share, normalized EBITDA, concentration, and owner dependence.
e.rationale: Traditional licensed distributors with repeat external retail accounts generally fit the lens, but eligibility is reduced by captive manufacturer channels, owner-dependent routes, concentration, thin economics, and electronic-cigarette businesses exposed to unauthorized inventory. FDA reported only 34 authorized e-cigarette products and devices as of December 2024 while documenting extensive enforcement across the supply chain, making compliance quality a central eligibility screen.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 424940 ($1,782,253,237) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 424940 ($148,431,788,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1137 (margins.json[42]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Retail (Distributors) EBITDA/Sales 11.37%); revenue/employee $2,540,857 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 424940); class <5: 780 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,345,471 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $380,380 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 249 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,116,438 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,377,639 -> IN $1-10M band; class 10-19: 178 firms, avg revenue/firm $24,664,545 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,804,359 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 163 firms, avg revenue/firm $62,967,031 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,159,351 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 61 firms, avg revenue/firm $242,616,885 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $27,585,540 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,397,703,952 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $613,718,939 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 590

caveats: Regulation preserves accountable functions but does not guarantee that an independent LMM wholesaler performs them.
The operator-required share is likely lower for national retail chains and higher for fragmented convenience and specialty stores.
o.change_evidence: Channel-flow data by product and retailer segment quantifying manufacturer-direct, large-retailer self-distribution, independent wholesaler, delivery-sale, and illicit volumes, plus interviews with manufacturers and retailers on functions they would internalize.
o.rationale: Surviving physical products still require accountable custody, inventory, credit, retailer delivery, returns, tax treatment, authorized-product controls, and records. ATF requires interstate distributors of cigarettes, ENDS, and smokeless tobacco to register and report, and federal rules require transaction records above specified thresholds. Software can automate parts of this chain, while manufacturer-direct delivery, large retailer self-distribution, and category contraction can remove the independent LMM operator.

caveats: FTC promotion data are manufacturer-side, dated, and specific to reporting e-cigarette companies rather than all 424940 contracts.
Capture can differ sharply among cigarettes, cigars, oral nicotine, authorized electronic cigarettes, and independent retail customers.
q.change_evidence: Customer- and manufacturer-level agreements and invoices showing price resets, rebates, promotional allowances, freight, excise-tax treatment, bid cadence, service fees, and realized gross-margin retention after measured productivity changes.
q.rationale: Wholesalers can retain some benefits from fewer errors, better inventory, lower bad debt, and more efficient route and compliance administration. Capture is limited by transparent branded products, powerful manufacturers and retail chains, thin spreads, frequent competitive repricing, and manufacturer-controlled discounts and allowances. FTC reported that price discounts, promotional allowances paid to wholesalers, and point-of-sale advertising together represented almost two thirds of e-cigarette promotional expenditure in 2021, indicating strong upstream influence on channel economics.

caveats: No direct source measures five-year AI implementation in compliant 424940 firms.
Regulatory work can be assisted but not safely eliminated without accountable review.
rho.change_evidence: Pre/post implementation data from distributor ERP, warehouse, route, trade-promotion, tax, and compliance systems showing straight-through processing, overrides, audit exceptions, labor hours, error credits, collections, and avoided hires for at least twelve months.
rho.rationale: Within five years, bounded automation should be implementable for orders, invoice exceptions, promotion claims, replenishment, route planning, collections, customer communications, and document assembly. Implementation is constrained by legacy distributor systems, customer-specific price books, multi-jurisdiction tax and licensing rules, authorization-list changes, auditability, and the need for human sign-off where inaccurate classification or sales could create enforcement exposure.

caveats: The observed transaction source covers all nondurable wholesalers and only deals reported to its marketplace.
No exact denominator, owner-age distribution, or failed-deal dataset exists for the eligible lens.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of 424940-matched closed deals from BizBuySell, Axial, GF Data, broker CRM systems, SBA 7(a) files, state license-transfer records, and owner interviews covering succession, compliance, supplier consents, and normalized margins.
s5.rationale: A five-year transfer probability below one fifth reflects a fragmented owner population and some succession supply, offset by license transfer, tax and regulatory diligence, supplier authorization, customer concentration, low margins, and heightened scrutiny of vape inventory. BizBuySell reported 16 nondurable-wholesaler transactions in 2025, showing transactability but not a tobacco-specific transfer rate.

principal_driver: Regulated, high-frequency retailer replenishment creates recurring operational and compliance workflows that can be made more accurate and efficient.
principal_weakness: The labor ratio is extremely low, while combustible demand declines and lawful electronic-cigarette supply remains exposed to abrupt enforcement and authorization changes.

run_meta.title: Tobacco Product and Electronic Cigarette Merchant Wholesalers

sources.supports: For the broader 4241/4247/4249 group, identifies leading occupations including 66,350 nontechnical sales representatives, 56,820 heavy-truck drivers, 45,270 material movers, 25,980 stockers/order fillers, 15,980 office clerks, and 15,630 customer-service representatives.
sources.title: BLS Data Tables for OEWS Industry Charts, May 2024

sources.supports: Lists wholesale-sales tasks including product, price and availability inquiries, recommendations, quotes, contracts and orders, market monitoring, sales reports, stock checks, customer contact, and order forwarding.
sources.title: O*NET OnLine: Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing, Except Technical and Scientific Products

sources.supports: Reports that cigarettes sold by the largest U.S. cigarette companies to wholesalers and retailers declined from 190.2 billion in 2021 to 173.5 billion in 2022.
sources.title: Federal Trade Commission: Tobacco Reports and Enforcement

sources.supports: Reports U.S. cigarette packs down about 27% from 12.5 billion in 2015 to 9.1 billion in 2021; nicotine lozenge, puck, and pouch sales rising from $452.76 million in 2020 to $1.06 billion in 2022; and monthly e-cigarette sales rising from $75 million in January 2017 to $469 million in March 2022.
sources.title: CDC: Economic Trends in Tobacco

sources.supports: Reports that 9.9% of U.S. adults used cigarettes and 7.0% used electronic cigarettes in 2024 and states that cigarette use has decreased while electronic-cigarette use has increased.
sources.title: CDC NCHS: Cigarette and Electronic Cigarette Use Among Adults by Urbanization Level, United States, 2024

sources.supports: As of December 19, 2024, reports 34 authorized e-cigarette products and devices, more than 700 warning letters to firms for manufacturing, selling, or distributing unauthorized new tobacco products, and more than 800 retailer warning letters.
sources.title: FDA Warns Nine Online Firms for Illegally Selling Flavored, Disposable E-Cigarettes

sources.supports: States that interstate distributors of cigarettes, ENDS, and smokeless tobacco must register and report to ATF and state tax administrators, and that delivery sellers face reporting, labeling, delivery, recordkeeping, licensing, tax, and stamping requirements.
sources.title: ATF: Prevent All Cigarette Trafficking Act

sources.supports: Reports 16 nondurable-goods wholesaler/distributor transactions in 2025, with median sale price $525,000, median revenue $1,301,710, and median cash flow $225,000.
sources.title: BizBuySell Insight Report Data Tables: Full-Year 2025

title: Tobacco Product and Electronic Cigarette Merchant Wholesalers
